We recommended and Management agreed to communicate with the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) to address the issue of the over, double, and erroneous remittances and ensure that the LGU does not incur any losses resulting therefrom and improve internal controls to prevent similar errors from occurring in the future.

We recommended and Management agreed that the BAC Chairman and the Secretariat immediately submit the documents required. Moving forward, the LGU should ensure that all developmental projects included in the AIP for the 20% Local Development Fund are implementation-ready and can be executed within a reasonable timeframe. This will help optimize fund utilization and ensure that allocated resources effectively contribute to local development.

Construction of storage of fuel is costly and a potential hazard. Also, the procurement of fuel through public bidding per quarter is not viable due to the fast movement of fuel prices.

We recommend that the Municipal Vice Mayor make a representation to the VMLP Bohol Chapter to have the amount paid as annual dues to the league be reviewed; taking into consideration the needs of each municipality, the pandemic that may have cancelled the activities planned for CY 2020, thus, expected to have left material unexpended fund balance of the VMLP; and suggest that it be lessened to pave way for other necessary expenditures of the respective agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the GAD Focal Person prepare and submit the GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2020 to the COA Auditor. observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit the necessary supporting documents to establish the propriety of the distribution of the medicines and medical and laboratory supplies such as inventory of the supplies at year-end and distribution list signed by the recipients. We recommend that management submit a written explanation why the Office of the Municipal Engineer is currently preparing the As-Built Plans of the identified projects, require submission of this plans from the contractors and furnish copy of the same to the undersigned. Furthermore, it is recommended that management require that final payments pertaining to completed infrastructure projects must be released only after the submission of all the required supporting documents in accordance with Section 4 (6) of P. observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit a formal letter explaining the material fuel consumption of the LGU. Furthermore, we reiterate the need to maintain monthly monitoring of fuel consumption in order to prevent wastage of government resources. Also, charging of fuel per department is recommended to be strictly monitored to ensure that the fuel consumption charged actually reflects every department’s usage.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee to properly plan ahead and determine the schedule for physical count per department in order to prioritize and fully realize the conduct of a complete physical count of the PPE and Inventories of the LGU. Likewise, we recommend that the Inventory Committee prepare the RPCPPE and RPCI and submit copies thereof to COA in order to verify compliance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs. observations.managementAction: Municipal Treasurer committed to submit the RPCPPE and RPCI on February 26, 2021 and to prospectively comply with the submission of these annual reports not later than January 31 each year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the accountable personnel concerned who are in active service to immediately liquidate their cash advances pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002. Require the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to those already separated from the LGU but can still be traced in coordination with the officers who gave them clearance from accountability. Moreover, consider requesting for write-off those unliquidated advances of deceased accountable personnel or those that can no longer be traced. Lastly, enforce settlement through deduction from the salary of the officials or employees who fail to comply with the requirements on the liquidation of cash advances.
We recommend that management immediately fill-up the four mandatory positions for the LDRRMO.

We recommend that management ensure all government-funded projects be implemented within its scheduled timeline indicated in the AIP so that the intended benefits of these projects will be enjoyed by the constituents and to get the maximum value of money in the utilization of the fund considering the increasing prices of materials and labor. Furthermore, conduct actual studies for previously identified projects and determine if these are still feasible and practicable to be implemented, otherwise, realign funds to other projects attune with the present needs and constraints.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries in order to correct the erroneous charging of prior year’s expenditures against the current year appropriation. Henceforth, management is advised to strictly comply with the aforementioned regulations.

We recommend that management henceforth adhere to the provision of Section 4 (3) of P. D. 1445, exhaust all means possible to ensure the delivery of the remaining funds intended to finance the two identified government projects for reimbursement of the fund used for the purpose other than that it was released. We recommend that management require the LDRRMC to monitor and evaluate the use and disbursements of the LDRRMF. Henceforth, maximize utilization of the pre-disaster preparedness programs and/or activities so that the intended purpose is attained/achieved as provided in Rule 18 of RA 10121.

Henceforth, require the BAC to follow the procedures stipulated in RA 9184 in the conduct of public bidding in all its procurement of goods, services and infrastructure project.
